title: Fix negated range extraction in lex DFA parser
description:
Comprehensive lex DFA pattern audit found 537 unhandled if-patterns across 15 grammars.

UNHANDLED (need fixes):
1. Exclusion ranges (lookahead < X || Y < lookahead) in compound conditions — 259 occurrences across 14 grammars. Root cause: line 304 || check in parseIfTransitions prevents entering negation branch.
2. lookahead > X (standalone + compound with exclusion ranges) — 120 occurrences across 4 grammars (Ruby 81, TS 14, JS 11).
3. !eof && set_contains() — 119 occurrences across 4 grammars (Bash 82, CSS 32, Perl 1, Python 4).
4. !eof && non-set_contains — 2 occurrences in Python only.

ALREADY HANDLED (by f3e081a):
- lookahead == 0xNN hex equality — handled by hexEqRe
- lookahead != 0xNN hex negation — handled by neqHexRe

Priority fix: Pattern 1 (exclusion ranges) is highest impact at 259 instances. Patterns 1 and 2 share root cause — the || bypass on line 304. Fix: parse (lookahead < X || Y < lookahead) as negated ranges within compound negation logic.

CONFIRMED ABSENT (no instances found):
>=, !set_contains, else/else-if, nested ifs, goto, for/while, ACCEPT_TOKEN inside if, additional switch.
